lede: A look at December 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jean-François Champollion and Chet Baker, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 3519
events.desc: After experiencing an engine fire, Aeroflot Flight 3519 attempts to make an emergency landing at Krasnoyarsk International Airport but crashes, killing 110 of the 111 people on board.

events.title: Democratic Republic of the Congo
events.desc: The Democratic Republic of the Congo officially becomes a one-party state.

events.title: Japan
events.desc: Seven Japanese military and political leaders convicted of war crimes by the International Military Tribunal for the Far East are executed by Allied occupation authorities at Sugamo Prison in Tokyo, Japan.

events.title: Transistor
events.desc: The transistor is first demonstrated at Bell Laboratories.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: After 15 days of fighting, the Imperial Japanese Army occupies Wake Island.

events.title: Colombia
events.desc: Colombia becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty.

events.title: Sex Disqualification (Removal) Act 1919
events.desc: Sex Disqualification (Removal) Act 1919 becomes law in the United Kingdom, allowing women to serve as lawyers, civil servants, and in other professions, as well as to serve on juries.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Australian and New Zealand troops arrive in Cairo, Egypt.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington resigns as commander-in-chief of the Continental Army at the Maryland State House in Annapolis, Maryland.

events.title: Yohl Ikʼnal
events.desc: Maya queen Yohl Ik'nal is crowned ruler of Palenque.

births.name: Chet Baker
births.line: American jazz trumpet player, flugelhorn player, and singer

births.name: Helmut Schmidt
births.line: German soldier, economist, and politician, 5th Chancellor of Germany

births.name: Emperor Zhenzong
births.line: of Song, emperor of the Song dynasty

deaths.text: Dési Bouterse, Surinamese general and politician, 9th President of Suriname
Kurt Meyer, German SS general and convicted war criminal
Thomas Robert Malthus, English economist and demographer

observances.text: Birthday of the Queen Silvia, an official flag flying day (Sweden)
Christian Feast Day: Behnam, Sarah, and the Forty Martyrs (Coptic Church)
